meta.name: Next.js 15 + Tauri 2 (Desktop App)
meta.description: Production-ready desktop application using Tauri 2 with Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, Zustand state management. Builds lightweight native binaries for Linux and macOS with Rust backend for system access, filesystem operations, and shell commands via Tauri's command system.

architecture.summary: A desktop application architecture using Tauri 2 with a Next.js frontend rendered in a native webview. The Rust backend handles privileged system operations (filesystem, shell, OS info) exposed to the frontend via Tauri's invoke command system. No Electron overhead — produces small, fast native binaries for Linux (.deb, .AppImage, .rpm) and macOS (.dmg, .app).

layers.name: Pages & Server Components (RSC)
layers.description: Next.js App Router pages using React Server Components for static rendering. In Tauri, RSC pages are pre-rendered at build time via static export. No Node.js server at runtime.
rules: No dynamic server-side rendering in Tauri
Layouts and pages can be server components
Data fetching at build time only for RSC

layers.name: Client Components (CMP)
layers.description: React 'use client' components that run in the Tauri webview. Handle user interaction, call Zustand stores for state, and invoke Tauri commands for system access.
rules: No direct Tauri invoke calls — go through stores
State comes from STO via hooks

layers.name: State Layer (STO)
layers.description: Zustand stores managing application state. Stores call Tauri invoke() for system operations. Single source of truth for all frontend state.
rules: Handles loading/error states for async commands

layers.name: Tauri Command Layer (CMD)
layers.description: Rust #[tauri::command] functions in the Tauri backend. Handle filesystem operations, shell commands, OS information, and other privileged system access. Invoked from the frontend via Tauri's IPC.
rules: Input validation in Rust before execution
Return Result<T, String> for error handling

layers.name: Rust Services (SVC)
layers.description: Rust modules containing business logic called by Tauri commands. Separate concerns from the command handler layer.

architecture.dataFlow: RSC (static) | CMP -> STO -> invoke() -> CMD (Rust) -> SVC (Rust) -> System/FS/Shell
keyPrinciples: Next.js runs as a static export — no Node.js server at runtime
Tauri webview loads the static Next.js build
All system access goes through Tauri invoke() command bridge
Rust commands are the ONLY bridge between frontend and system
Tauri's allowlist controls which APIs are exposed to the webview
Filesystem access scoped via Tauri's fs scope permissions
Small binary size — Tauri uses system webview, not bundled Chromium
Cross-platform builds for Linux and macOS via cargo and Tauri CLI
GitHub Actions CI/CD for automated release builds on both platforms
architecture.securityModel: Tauri's capability-based permission system controls which commands, APIs, and filesystem paths are accessible from the webview. Commands are explicitly registered. No arbitrary code execution from the frontend. CSP headers restrict script sources.
architecture.buildProcess: Next.js builds static export to out/. Tauri bundles the static output into a native app shell. cargo build compiles the Rust backend. tauri build produces platform installers: .deb/.AppImage/.rpm for Linux, .dmg/.app for macOS.

app._note: Next.js App Router pages and layouts

stores._note: STO: Zustand stores calling Tauri invoke

tauri._note: Typed wrappers around Tauri invoke commands

files.client/package.json:
{
  "name": "nextjs-tauri-app",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "tauri": "tauri",
    "tauri:dev": "tauri dev",
    "tauri:build": "tauri build",
    "tauri:build-linux": "tauri build --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu",
    "tauri:build-mac": "tauri build --target universal-apple-darwin"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "next": "^15.0.0",
    "zustand": "^5.0.0",
    "@tauri-apps/api": "^2.0.0",
    "@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog": "^2.0.0",
    "@tauri-apps/plugin-fs": "^2.0.0",
    "@tauri-apps/plugin-shell": "^2.0.0",
    "@tauri-apps/plugin-os": "^2.0.0",
    "@tauri-apps/plugin-clipboard-manager": "^2.0.0",
    "@tauri-apps/plugin-notification": "^2.0.0",
    "@tauri-apps/plugin-updater": "^2.0.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.0",
    "tailwind-merge": "^3.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@tauri-apps/cli": "^2.0.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.0",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.0.0",
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": "^4.0.0",
    "postcss": "^8.5.0",
    "eslint": "^9.0.0",
    "eslint-config-next": "^15.0.0"
  }
}
files.server/Cargo.toml:
[package]
name = "nextjs-tauri-app"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.77"

[build-dependencies]
tauri-build = { version = "2", features = [] }

[dependencies]
tauri = { version = "2", features = [] }
tauri-plugin-dialog = "2"
tauri-plugin-fs = "2"
tauri-plugin-shell = "2"
tauri-plugin-os = "2"
tauri-plugin-clipboard-manager = "2"
tauri-plugin-notification = "2"
tauri-plugin-updater = "2"
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
files..github/workflows/release.yml:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  build-linux:
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install system dependencies
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev libappindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev patchelf

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: client/package-lock.json

      - name: Setup Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - name: Rust cache
        uses: swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          workspaces: server

      - name: Install frontend dependencies
        run: cd client && npm ci

      - name: Build Tauri app
        uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          projectPath: server
          tagName: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          releaseName: "Release ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          releaseBody: "See the assets for download links."
          releaseDraft: true
          prerelease: false

  build-macos:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: client/package-lock.json

      - name: Setup Rust
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          targets: aarch64-apple-darwin,x86_64-apple-darwin

      - name: Rust cache
        uses: swatinem/rust-cache@v2
        with:
          workspaces: server

      - name: Install frontend dependencies
        run: cd client && npm ci

      - name: Build Tauri app (universal)
        uses: tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          projectPath: server
          args: --target universal-apple-darwin
          tagName: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          releaseName: "Release ${{ github.ref_name }}"
          releaseBody: "See the assets for download links."
          releaseDraft: true
          prerelease: false

CMP.description: React Client Component (runs in Tauri webview)

STO.description: Zustand store (calls Tauri invoke for system access)

SVC.description: Rust service module with business logic
